metadata.title:
LAYING RAILS IN
BIG CASCADE:


TUNNEL

pages.raw_text:
Without delaying train service
the Great Northern is laying new
S5-pound steel rails through the
Cascade tunnel. Track crews run
in and out between trains working
when they can by torch'iGht. 1t IS
expected that the three miles of
tunnel will be equipped with new
rails throughout insid of six weeks.
White men at first refused to work
in the tunnel and Japs were tried,
but they could not do the work and
white crews were secured by re-
laying the rails at night and pay
ing overtime. The work Is done at
night in order to escape all the
traffic possible. Operations are
greatly hindered by the suffocating
smoke that remains in the tunnel
half an hour after train has
passed through.
